headline: Merck buying Terns in $6.7B deal to bolster its cancer portfolio before key Keytruda patent expires
summary: Merck is buying oncology company Terns Pharmaceuticals in a deal valued at approximately $6.7 billion as the pharmaceutical giant works on beefing up its cancer portfolio before a key patent on its cancer drug Keytruda expires in two years.  Merck received accelerated approval for Keytruda from the Food and Drug Administration in September 2014 to treat advanced or unresectable melanoma.  The drug has since been approved to treat more than 15 types of cancers and has been a key contributor to Merck's revenue.

The drug has since been approved to treat more than 15 types of cancers and has been a key contributor to Merck's revenue.

headline: Merck to buy oncology firm Terns Pharmaceuticals in $6.7 billion deal
summary: Investing.com -- Pharmaceutical giant Merck announced Wednesday that it will acquire clinical-stage oncology company Terns Pharmaceuticals for $53.00 per share in cash, representing an approximate equity value of $6.7 billion. The deal equates to approximately $5.7 billion net of acquired cash and represents a premium of 31% to the 60-day and 42% to the 90-day volume-weighted average stock price on Tuesday.
